_audit_note: Hand audit of every candidate drift pair the sweep's union of cells surfaces on the real corpora (superbot-next@af985c1 sb/ vs superbot@1ecc211 disbot/) that is not mechanically auto-classifiable. One auditor read the source context on BOTH sides of every pair (file:line in results.json 'pairs'). Verdict semantics: 'true-drift' = the rebuild literal is user-facing copy under the oracle-verbatim contract and byte-differs from its oracle source -- a defect a red gate should stop; 'intentional-change' = the correspondence is real but the byte difference is deliberate or render-equivalent (SQL reflow, f-string assembly splits, repr-vs-quotes, mention-formatting) -- gate-FP, waiver class; 'false-pair' = coincidental normalized collision between different objects (identifier vs copy, fragment vs key, usage string vs command name) -- gate-FP, noise class. Pairs whose rebuild literal byte-matches the oracle pool somewhere are auto-classified false-pair by the runner (mechanically derivable; the r-noexact gating rule's own semantics) and are NOT listed here.

pairs.reason: N is the f-string fragment of an app-command group description f"{name} commands"; O sites are dict keys / keyword hints -- different objects.
N is an f-string fragment of an operator finding detail; O sites are status-enum keys -- different objects.
N is a usage display string ('!'+name); O is the command registration identifier -- rendered surface equal, not copy drift.
N is a dataset-label f-string fragment (trailing space before {version}); O is source-attribution prose in a different sentence -- fragment boundary artifact, different copy objects.
